column2: in tē spērāvī~; salvum mē fac ex omnibus persequentibus mē
column5: in thee have I put my trust~; save me from all them that persecute me

column1: 3 ^ nēquandō rapiat ut leō animam meam
column3: neque quī salvum faciat. ^ Lest at any time he seize upon my soul like a lion
column4: while there is no one to redeem me

column3: sī est inīquitās in manibus meīs
column6: if I have done this thing
column7: if there be iniquity in my hands

column2: dēcidam meritō ab inimīcīs meīs inānis. ^ if I have rendered to them that repaid me evils
column3: let me deservedly fall empty before my enemies.
et conculcet in terrā vītam meam
column4: et glōriam meam in pulverem dēdūcat. ^ Let the enemy pursue my soul
column8: and bring down my glory to the dust.

column3: in īrā tuā~; exaltāre in fīnibus inimīcōrum meōrum
column6: in praeceptō quod mandāvistī. ^ Rise up
column8: in thy anger~; be thou exalted in the borders of my enemies
column11: in the precept which thou hast commanded.

column2: et propter hanc in altum regredere. ^ And a congregation of people shall surround thee
column3: and for their sakes return thou on high.

column4: et secundum innocentiam meam super mē. ^ The Lord judgeth the people. Judge me
column7: and according to my innocence in me.

column3: scrūtans corda et rēnēs Deus. ^ The wickedness of sinners shall be brought to nought
column4: and thou shalt direct the just
column5: the searcher of hearts and reins is God.

column2: quī salvōs facit rēctōs corde. ^ Just is my help from the Lord~; who saveth the upright of heart.

column3: et patiens~; ¿~numquid īrāscitur per singulōs diēs~? ^ God is a just judge
column4: strong and patient~; whether he be angred throughout each day~?

column2: gladium suum vibrābit~; arcum suum tetendit
column3: et parāvit illum. ^ Except you will be converted
column4: he will brandish his sword~; he hath bent his bow

column2: sagittās suās ardentibus effēcit. ^ And in it he hath prepared to instruments of death
column3: he hath made ready his arrows for them that burn.

column2: et peperit inīquitātem. ^ Behold he hath been in labour with injustice~; he hath conceived sorrow

column4: quam fēcit. ^ He hath opened a pit and dug it
column5: and he is fallen into the hole

column1: 17 ^ Convertētur dolor ejus in caput ejus

column2: et psallem nōminī Dominī altissimī. ^ I will give glory to the Lord according to his justice
